what_it_does: An oxlint configuration that enforces design-system adherence: it forbids importing design-system component internals (only the package index.js entry is allowed), bans raw hex colors / raw px values / non-system fonts in literals, and uses AST selectors to restrict each design-system component (Avatar, Badge, Button, Card, ContainmentTree, DiagramSolver, Glyphic, Input, Modal, CVType, SegmentedOption, Slide, StepItem, Switch, TabItem) to its declared props and enumerated prop value sets.
observations: Header note in the consuming CLAUDE.md says this file is compiled output and must never be hand-edited.

no-restricted-imports lists a large explicit group of internal paths (app/**, axes/**, core/**, components/**, ui_kits/**, etc.) all funnelling to the message 'Import design-system components from index.js, not component internals.'
no-restricted-syntax uses regex Literal selectors to flag raw hex (#RRGGBB[AA]), raw px values, and font-family declarations not using Sora/DM Sans/JetBrains Mono.
Per-component JSXOpeningElement selectors whitelist allowed prop names (always including key/ref/className/style/children) and reject anything else with a component-specific message.
Per-prop selectors pin enumerated value sets, e.g. Button variant ∈ {primary,ink,outline,ghost,soft,comm,default}, Glyphic form ∈ 9 shapes, Card variant ∈ {soft,surface,outline,gold}.
An overrides block turns off no-restricted-imports for **/index.js (the allowed re-export barrel).

Carries a non-standard 'x-omelette' payload: a components map (each with an empty 'replaces' array), a flat list of ~470 design tokens, a tokenKinds map classifying each token (color/spacing/shadow/font/radius/other), and a fontFamilies list.
standouts: The x-omelette block embeds the entire token catalogue (~470 tokens) plus a token→kind classification inside a lint config — the lint config doubles as a machine-readable token manifest.
Component prop contracts (allowed props + enumerated values) are encoded as oxlint AST selectors, turning the design-system API surface into lint-enforceable rules.
conventions: Token-driven: raw hex/px/fonts are disallowed in favour of var() tokens.
Single-entry import discipline: consume from index.js, never component internals.
Closed enumerations: component prop values are restricted to named sets (fail-on-unknown-prop / fail-on-unknown-value).
Generated-not-edited: treated as compiled output per the project charter.
Carries an extension namespace (x-omelette) to ride extra registry data inside a standard config schema.
concerns: react/forbid-elements is configured with an empty forbid list — currently a no-op rule.
Every component entry's 'replaces' array is empty — the field looks declared-but-unpopulated.

tokenKinds has classifications that look mismatched for several entries (e.g. --frame-desktop-h / --page-w / --gutter mapped to kind 'color', --_pct and --zone-pigment mapped to 'color') which may be classifier artefacts rather than true kinds.
All rules are 'warn', so violations do not fail a build by themselves.
notes: A generated oxlint config that hard-codes the design system's import discipline, no-raw-literal rules, and per-component prop/value contracts — and smuggles the full token catalogue in an x-omelette extension block.
questions: What tool produces this file and the x-omelette block (the empty 'replaces' arrays suggest a planned component-replacement/migration feature)?
Why are rules set to 'warn' rather than 'error' if adherence is meant to be enforced?
Are the tokenKinds entries that read 'color' for px/spacing-looking tokens intentional or a generation bug?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the filename '_adherence' and contents both serve enforcing adherence to the design system's tokens, imports, and component contracts.
intention_signals: Inferred: this is meant to be the machine-enforced guardrail of the 'everything defined once, referenced everywhere' principle — it tries to make drift (raw values, internal imports, off-contract props) detectable by a linter, and embeds the token registry so the same config that lints also describes the legal vocabulary.
novelty: Encoding a whole design-system component API (allowed props + enumerated values) as oxlint AST selectors, and embedding the complete token catalogue with kind-classification inside the lint config via a custom x-omelette key, is unusual — the lint config IS a design-system manifest.
contribution: Brings a machine-readable contract layer: the exact legal prop names and value enumerations per component, the import-discipline boundary, the no-raw-literal rules, and a flat token catalogue with kind classification — a deterministic adherence/vocabulary spec a fused system could query or enforce against.
summary_for_embedding: Generated oxlint config enforcing design-system adherence — banning raw hex/px/non-system fonts and component-internal imports, restricting each component to declared props and enumerated values, with the full ~470-token catalogue and token-kind map embedded in an x-omelette extension.
intention_for_embedding: Make design-system drift lint-detectable by encoding the import boundary, no-raw-literal rules, and per-component prop/value contracts as enforceable rules, while carrying the canonical token vocabulary inside the same config.
